title: Check open ports
text: Use: `netstat -tulnp` or `ss -tulnp` to see listening services

title: Restart Service
text: Use `systemctl restart <service>` to restart a service after configuration changes.

title: Check disk usage
text: `df -h` shows disk usage in human readable format

title: Check logs live
text: `journalctl -f` is your best friend

title: Ping test
text: Use `ping 8.8.8.8` to test internet connectivity

title: Check active connections
text: `ss -tunap` gives detailed network connections

title: Check Failed SSH Logins
text: Use `grep Failed /var/log/auth.log` to identify brute-force attempts.

title: Find Top Memory Consumers
text: Use `ps aux --sort=-%mem | head` to find processes using the most RAM.

title: Find Top CPU Consumers
text: Use `ps aux --sort=-%cpu | head` to identify CPU-intensive processes.

title: Monitor Network Traffic
text: Use `iftop` or `nload` to view live bandwidth usage.

title: Check Open Files
text: Use `lsof` to see which files are currently opened by processes.

title: Test DNS Resolution
text: Use `dig google.com` or `nslookup google.com` to verify DNS functionality.

title: View Routing Table
text: Use `ip route` to inspect network routes.

title: Check Firewall Rules
text: Use `iptables -L -n -v` or `nft list ruleset` to inspect firewall policies.

title: Verify Time Synchronization
text: Use `timedatectl status` to check NTP synchronization.

title: Check Disk I/O
text: Use `iostat -xz 1` to monitor disk performance.

title: Analyze Boot Time
text: Use `systemd-analyze blame` to find slow startup services.

title: List Listening Ports
text: Use `ss -lntp` instead of the deprecated netstat.

title: Check System Uptime
text: Use `uptime` to quickly see load averages and uptime.

title: Monitor Logs Live
text: Use `journalctl -xef` to follow logs and view recent errors.

title: Check TCP Connections
text: Use `ss -ant` to inspect active TCP sessions.

title: Find Largest Directories
text: Use `du -sh * | sort -hr` to locate storage hogs.

title: Test HTTP Response
text: Use `curl -I https://example.com` to inspect headers.

title: Check SSL Certificate
text: Use `openssl s_client -connect domain.com:443` to inspect TLS details.

title: Verify Cron Jobs
text: Use `crontab -l` to review scheduled user tasks.

title: Check Swap Usage
text: Use `swapon --show` to view active swap devices.

title: Inspect Mounted Filesystems
text: Use `findmnt` for a cleaner view than `mount`.

title: Monitor Real-Time Processes
text: Use `htop` for an interactive process viewer.

title: Check Package Updates
text: Regularly `update systems using apt, dnf, pacman`, or your distro package manager.

title: Secure SSH
text: `Disable password authentication` and `use SSH keys` whenever possible.

title: Limit Root Access
text: Prefer `sudo` over `direct root` logins for accountability.

title: Enable Fail2Ban
text: `Fail2Ban` helps block repeated failed login attempts.

title: Backup Before Updating
text: Always `create backups` before major upgrades or configuration changes.

title: Check Memory Pressure
text: Use `vmstat 1` to inspect memory and CPU pressure.

title: Find Zombie Processes
text: Use `ps aux | grep Z` to locate zombie processes.

title: Check Established Connections
text: Use `ss -ant state established` to list active TCP sessions.

title: Inspect DNS Records
text: Use `dig ANY domain.com` cautiously to inspect DNS information.

title: Verify Reverse DNS
text: Use `dig -x <IP>` to check PTR records.

title: Test Latency
text: Use `ping` to measure round-trip times.

title: Trace Network Paths
text: Use `traceroute` or `mtr` to diagnose routing issues.

title: Check System Temperature
text: Use `sensors` from lm-sensors to monitor hardware temperatures.

title: View Kernel Version
text: Use `uname -r` to identify the running kernel.

title: Check Listening UDP Ports
text: Use `ss -lun` to view UDP services.

title: Verify Service Status
text: Use `systemctl status <service>` for detailed service diagnostics.

title: Check Failed Services
text: Use `systemctl --failed` to find broken services.

title: Monitor Login Sessions
text: Use `who` or `w` to view active users.

title: Audit Recent Logins
text: Use `last` to inspect login history.

title: Check File Permissions
text: Use `namei -l <path>` to inspect permission chains.

title: Verify Public IP
text: Use `curl ifconfig.me` or similar services.

title: Inspect ARP Table
text: Use `ip neigh` to view ARP and neighbor entries.

title: Check Network Interfaces
text: Use `ip addr show` to inspect interface configurations.

title: Test Download Speed
text: Use `speedtest-cli` or `iperf3` for network performance tests.

title: Monitor Bandwidth Usage
text: Use `vnstat` to collect long-term traffic statistics.

title: Check Certificate Expiry
text: Monitor `TLS certificates` to avoid unexpected outages.

title: Rotate Logs
text: Ensure `logrotate` is configured to prevent disk exhaustion.

title: Document Changes
text: `Keep notes` of configuration changes to simplify troubleshooting.
